description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped stone with a predominantly gray coloration interspersed with lighter and darker mineral streaks. The surface is rough and uneven, with visible grain and a natural, unpolished texture. One side of the stone features a small, hand-written label, suggesting it may have been cataloged or collected for study. The stone's edges are jagged and angular, with some faces appearing more planar while others are more rugged and fractured. Subtle hints of yellowish and brownish tones are present, likely due to mineral inclusions or weathering. The overall appearance is that of a naturally occurring rock, possibly granite or gneiss, suitable for use as an urban obstacle or landscape feature.
description: A rough, gray stone with jagged edges, mineral streaks, and a hand-written label on one side. Its surface is unpolished and naturally textured.
description_view_0: This view shows the broadest, flattest face of the stone, highlighting its mottled gray and white coloration and rough texture.
description_view_1: This angle reveals a more angular, pointed end of the stone and emphasizes the uneven, fractured surface.
description_view_2: This side displays the hand-written label on a relatively flat face, with visible layering and a mix of gray and yellowish hues.
description_view_3: This perspective shows the elongated, striated surface of the stone, with pronounced grain and a slightly more uniform coloration.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or partially embedded in the ground
attribute_pattern: Irregular mottling and subtle mineral veining, with no artificial carvings or regular patterns.
attribute_weathering: Rough and unpolished, with minor discoloration suggesting moderate exposure to the elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Blends naturally with soil, gravel, or other stones, but may contrast with concrete or metal urban features.
